tool_name: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST)

inputs.name: Net Shift Available Time
inputs.hint: Gross shift minus: planned breaks, cleaning, TPM. Typical: 450 min (8h shift – 30 min break)

inputs.name: Station 1 — Standard Minute Value (SMV)
inputs.hint: From time study, MTM-2, or MOST — includes allowances (fatigue, personal, delay)

inputs.name: Operator Allowance (fatigue + personal + delay)
inputs.hint: ILO: 10–20% for light assembly | Heavy manual: 20–30% | MTM standard: 15%

formulas: Takt_Time_min = shift_net_time_min / (daily_demand_units / num_shifts)   // [min/unit] Required cycle rate — pace of customer demand
Total_SMV_min = station_1_smv + station_2_smv + station_3_smv + station_4_smv + station_5_smv + station_6_smv + station_7_smv + station_8_smv   // [min/unit] Sum of all station work content
Bottleneck_SMV_min = MAX(station_1_smv, station_2_smv, station_3_smv, station_4_smv, station_5_smv, station_6_smv, station_7_smv, station_8_smv)   // [min/unit] Constraining station — sets line rate
Theoretical_Min_Operators = Total_SMV_min / Takt_Time_min   // [persons] Minimum operators to meet takt
Balance_Delay_pct = 100 - Line_Efficiency_pct   // [%] Idle time fraction across all stations
Actual_Output_per_Shift = shift_net_time_min / Bottleneck_SMV_min   // [units/shift] Maximum achievable at bottleneck
Daily_Output_Capacity = Actual_Output_per_Shift * num_shifts   // [units/day] Total daily capacity

Labour_Cost_per_Unit_USD = (actual_operators * (shift_net_time_min / 60) * labour_rate_usd_hr * num_shifts) / MIN(Daily_Output_Capacity, daily_demand_units)   // [USD/unit] Labour cost per produced unit
Idle_Operator_Equivalent = actual_operators - Theoretical_Min_Operators   // [persons] Excess operators vs theoretical minimum
Annual_Labour_Waste_USD = Idle_Operator_Equivalent * labour_rate_usd_hr * shift_net_time_min / 60 * num_shifts * 250   // [USD/year] Cost of imbalance waste
SMV_per_Operator = Total_SMV_min / actual_operators   // [min] Average work content per operator

standards: IE Line Balancing (Kilbridge & Wester / RPW Method)

daily_demand_positive.error_msg: Daily demand must be at least 1 unit. Zero demand makes line balancing undefined.

station_smv_minimum.error_msg: Station 1 SMV must be greater than zero — at least one workstation must have content.

operators_minimum.error_msg: At least 1 operator is required on the line.

smart_warnings.message: TAKT VIOLATION: Bottleneck station cycle time exceeds takt time. Meeting daily demand target is MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE without: (1) splitting the bottleneck task, (2) adding a parallel station, or (3) increasing shift time. Calculate: required output deficit = (Bottleneck_SMV - Takt) × demand units.

LOW LINE EFFICIENCY (<75%): Over 25% of operator time is idle due to imbalance. Rebalance by: (1) relocating work elements from overloaded to underloaded stations, (2) applying RPW or Kilbridge-Wester algorithm, (3) combining or splitting stations. Target: >85% for high-volume lines.

EXCESS OPERATORS IDENTIFIED: Line has more operators than theoretical minimum. Each excess operator represents avoidable annual labour cost. Conduct kaizen event to redistribute work elements before adding headcount.

CAPACITY SHORTFALL: Line cannot meet daily demand at current bottleneck rate. Immediate actions: (1) Overtime authorisation, (2) Bottleneck relief (parallel resource), (3) Demand smoothing with customer. Quantify units short per day and escalate to production manager.

Low allowance (<10%): ILO recommends minimum 10% for light assembly work. Insufficient allowance leads to: (1) fatigue-induced quality defects, (2) absenteeism increase, (3) operator injury claims. Verify time study method includes all required rest periods.

VERY SHORT TAKT (<0.5 min): Cycle time under 30 seconds. Ergonomic risk of repetitive strain injury is high at this pace. EU Directive 2002/44/EC requires risk assessment. Consider task variety rotation and automation for highly repetitive operations.
